option.name: fileSummaryMaps
option.value: {"/home/rael/BACKUPs/ANDROID/APPS_EM_DESENVOLVIMENTO/PUBLICO/TimeJob/TimeJob":"{\"/app/src/main/res/mipmap-anydpi-v26/ic_launcher.xml\":\"This file is an XML configuration for an Android adaptive icon. It does not contain any classes or functions, but rather defines the background, foreground, and monochrome elements of the adaptive icon.\\n\\n`adaptive-icon`: This element defines the adaptive icon configuration, which allows the icon to be displayed correctly on different Android devices and screen sizes.\\n\\n`background`: This element specifies the drawable resource to be used as the background of the adaptive icon.\\n\\n`foreground`: This element specifies the drawable resource to be used as the foreground of the adaptive icon.\\n\\n`monochrome`: This element specifies the drawable resource to be used as the monochrome version of the adaptive icon, which is used in certain contexts such as the app launcher.\",\"/app/google-services.json\":\"This file appears to be a JSON configuration file for a Firebase project. It does not contain any classes or functions, but rather provides information about the project, such as the project number, project ID, and storage bucket. The file also includes client information, such as the mobile SDK app ID and the Android client package name. Additionally, it includes an API key and information about the app invite service. Overall, this file is used to configure the Firebase project and its associated services.\",\"/app/src/main/java/net/developermaster/timejob/view/ActivityAdicionar.kt\":\"`ActivityAdicionar`: This class is the main activity for the \\\"Adicionar\\\" (Add) feature of the TimeJob application.\\n\\n`onCreate`: This function is the entry point of the `ActivityAdicionar` class, where the activity is set up and the UI is inflated using Compose.\\n\\n`ComponentsActivityAdicionar().HomeAdicionar`: This function is responsible for rendering the UI components for the \\\"Adicionar\\\" feature, including a background color and other layout elements.\\n\\nThis file sets up the main activity for the \\\"Adicionar\\\" feature of the TimeJob application, using Jetpack Compose to create the user interface.\",\"/app/src/main/java/net/developermaster/timejob/view/MainActivity.kt\":\"This file contains the `MainActivity` class, which is the main entry point for the Android application. It sets up the UI using Jetpack Compose and initializes the `ComponentsMainActivity` class, which likely contains the main application logic and UI components.\\n\\n`MainActivity`: The main activity class that sets up the UI and initializes the application.\\n\\n`ComponentsMainActivity().Scaffold()`: This function likely sets up the main application layout and structure using Jetpack Compose.\\n\\nThe file does not contain any other classes or functions. It is focused on setting up the main activity and initializing the application\\u0027s UI components.\",\"/app/src/main/AndroidManifest.xml\":\"This file is an Android application manifest file that defines the configuration and settings for the application. It does not contain any classes or functions, but rather specifies the following:\\n\\n`MainActivity`: The main activity of the application, which is the entry point for the app and is set as the launcher activity.\\n\\n`ActivityAdicionar`: An additional activity in the application, which is likely used for adding or modifying data.\\n\\nThe manifest file also sets various application-level properties, such as the app\\u0027s name, icon, theme, and data extraction and backup rules.\",\"/app/src/main/res/drawable/ic_launcher_foreground.xml\":\"This file appears to be an Android vector drawable resource file. It defines a vector graphic that can be used as an app icon or other visual asset in an Android application. The file does not contain any classes or functions, but rather defines the vector path and gradient fill for the graphic.\\n\\nThe file defines two paths:\\n\\n1. The first path defines the main shape of the graphic, which appears to be a stylized \\\"M\\\" or \\\"W\\\" shape. The path is filled with a gradient that fades from a semi-transparent black to fully transparent.\\n\\n2. The second path defines the white \\\"fill\\\" of the graphic, which appears to be two rounded rectangles.\\n\\nOverall, this file provides a vector-based graphic that can be used as an app icon or other visual asset in an Android application, with the ability to scale the graphic without losing quality.\",\"/app/src/main/java/net/developermaster/timejob/utils/mensagemToast.kt\":\"This file does not contain any classes. It contains the following functions:\\n\\n`mensagemToast`: This function creates a custom Toast message with a black background, white text, and a larger font size, and displays it at the center of the screen.\\n\\nThe file appears to be a utility class that provides a custom Toast message functionality for the `MainActivity` class in the `net.developermaster.timejob.view` package.\",\"/app/src/main/java/net/developermaster/timejob/model/ModelTimeJob.kt\":\"This file contains a single data class:\\n\\n`ModelTimeJob`: A data class that represents a time job, including the date, entry time, exit time, and tips.\\n\\nThis file does not contain any functions. It simply defines a data class to represent a time job, which can be used in other parts of the application.\",\"/app/src/main/res/xml/data_extraction_rules.xml\":\"This file is an XML configuration file for data extraction rules in Android 12 backup and restore functionality. It does not contain any classes or functions, but rather provides a template for configuring what data should be included or excluded from cloud backups and device transfers.\\n\\nThe file contains the following sections:\\n\\n`\\u003ccloud-backup\\u003e`: This section is used to configure what data should be included or excluded from cloud backups.\\n\\n`\\u003cdevice-transfer\\u003e`: This section is used to configure what data should be included or excluded from device transfers.\\n\\nThe file is intended to be customized by developers to control what data is backed up or transferred when using the Android 12 backup and restore functionality.\",\"/app/src/main/res/xml/backup_rules.xml\":\"This file is an XML document that provides a sample backup rules file for Android applications. It is used to configure the automatic backup feature in Android, which allows users to restore their app data and settings on a new device.\\n\\nThe file does not contain any classes or functions. Instead, it provides a set of comments and example configurations for the `\\u003cfull-backup-content\\u003e` element, which is used to specify the data that should be included or excluded from the backup.\\n\\nThe main purpose of this file is to serve as a template for developers to customize the backup rules for their Android applications. By uncommented and modifying the example configurations, developers can control which data is backed up and which data is excluded from the backup process.\",\"/app/src/main/java/net/developermaster/timejob/view/ui/theme/Color.kt\":\"This file defines a set of color values for a UI theme in an Android Compose application. It does not contain any classes or functions, but rather defines a set of color constants that can be used throughout the application\\u0027s UI.\\n\\n`Purple80`: A dark purple color.\\n`PurpleGrey80`: A dark purple-grey color.\\n`Pink80`: A dark pink color.\\n`Purple40`: A lighter purple color.\\n`PurpleGrey40`: A lighter purple-grey color.\\n`Pink40`: A lighter pink color.\",\"/app/src/main/res/values/themes.xml\":\"This file does not contain any classes or functions. It is an XML resource file that defines a theme for an Android application. The theme is named \\\"Theme.TimeJob\\\" and it is based on the \\\"android:Theme.Material.Light.NoActionBar\\\" theme, which provides a light-colored material design theme without an action bar.\",\"/app/src/androidTest/java/net/developermaster/timejob/ExampleInstrumentedTest.kt\":\"This file contains an instrumented test for an Android app. It does not define any classes or functions, but rather tests the context of the app under test.\\n\\n`ExampleInstrumentedTest`: This is an instrumented test class that runs on an Android device and checks if the package name of the app under test matches the expected value.\\n\\n`useAppContext()`: This function retrieves the context of the app under test and asserts that the package name matches the expected value.\",\"/settings.gradle.kts\":\"This file is a Gradle configuration file for an Android project named \\\"TimeJob\\\". It sets up the plugin management and dependency resolution management for the project. There are no classes or functions defined in this file. Instead, it configures the following:\\n\\n`pluginManagement`: Specifies the repositories to be used for resolving Gradle plugins, including Google, Maven Central, and the Gradle Plugin Portal.\\n\\n`dependencyResolutionManagement`: Configures the repositories to be used for resolving project dependencies, including Google and Maven Central. It also sets the repositories mode to `FAIL_ON_PROJECT_REPOS`, which means that the build will fail if any project-specific repositories are defined.\\n\\n`rootProject.name`: Sets the name of the root project to \\\"TimeJob\\\".\\n\\n`include(\\\":app\\\")`: Includes the \\\":app\\\" module in the project.\",\"/app/src/main/res/drawable/ic_launcher_background.xml\":\"This file appears to be an Android vector drawable XML file, which defines a vector image that can be used as an app icon or other graphical element in an Android app. The file does not contain any classes or functions, but rather defines a set of paths and lines that make up the vector image.\\n\\nThe file defines a 108x108 dp vector image with a green background and a grid of white lines. The grid lines are defined using a series of `\\u003cpath\\u003e` elements, each of which specifies the start and end points of a line segment, as well as the stroke width and color.\\n\\nOverall, this file provides a vector-based representation of a simple grid-like design that can be used as an app icon or other graphical element in an Android app.\",\"/app/src/main/res/mipmap-anydpi-v26/ic_launcher_round.xml\":\"This file is an XML configuration for an Android adaptive icon. It does not contain any classes or functions, but rather defines the background, foreground, and monochrome elements of the adaptive icon.\\n\\n`adaptive-icon`: This element defines the adaptive icon configuration, which allows the icon to be displayed correctly on different Android devices and screen sizes.\\n\\n`background`: This element specifies the drawable resource to be used as the background of the adaptive icon.\\n\\n`foreground`: This element specifies the drawable resource to be used as the foreground of the adaptive icon.\\n\\n`monochrome`: This element specifies the drawable resource to be used as the monochrome version of the adaptive icon, which is used in certain contexts such as the app launcher.\",\"/app/src/main/java/net/developermaster/timejob/core/ComponentsActivityAdicionar.kt\":\"This file appears to be a Kotlin code for an Android Compose UI component. It does not contain any classes, but it has the following functions:\\n\\n`HomeAdicionar`: This function creates a column layout that contains a `Salvar` function from the `ComponentsFireBase` class.\\n\\n`ComponentsFireBase().Salvar()`: This function is responsible for saving something, but the specific functionality is not clear from the provided code.\\n\\nOverall, this file seems to be a part of a larger application that deals with time-related tasks and utilizes Firebase for data storage and management.\",\"/app/build.gradle.kts\":\"This file is a Gradle build script for an Android application. It sets up the project configuration, dependencies, and plugins required for the application. The file does not contain any classes or functions, but rather configures the build process for the Android application.\\n\\nThe key elements of the file are:\\n\\n`plugins`: Specifies the Android application plugin and the Kotlin Android plugin, as well as the Google Services plugin for Firebase integration.\\n\\n`android`: Configures the Android-specific settings, such as the namespace, compile SDK version, minimum and target SDK versions, and build types.\\n\\n`dependencies`: Specifies the dependencies for the application, including Android Core KTX, Lifecycle, Compose, Material 3, and Firebase Firestore and Analytics.\\n\\nThe file sets up the necessary configuration and dependencies for an Android application that uses Jetpack Compose and integrates with Firebase services.\",\"/app/src/main/res/values/colors.xml\":\"This file does not contain any classes or functions. It is an XML resource file that defines a set of color values for an Android application. The colors defined in this file can be used throughout the application to provide a consistent visual style.\",\"/app/src/main/java/net/developermaster/timejob/view/ui/theme/Theme.kt\":\"This file defines the theme for the TimeJob app using Jetpack Compose. It includes the following:\\n\\n`DarkColorScheme`: A private color scheme for the dark theme.\\n\\n`LightColorScheme`: A private color scheme for the light theme.\\n\\n`TimeJobTheme`: A composable function that sets the app\\u0027s theme based on the system\\u0027s dark mode setting and the device\\u0027s Android version.\\n\\nThis file is responsible for providing a consistent and customizable visual style for the TimeJob app across different device configurations and user preferences.\",\"/app/src/main/res/values/strings.xml\":\"This file appears to be an XML resource file that defines string resources for an Android application. It does not contain any classes or functions. The file defines two string resources:\\n\\n`app_name`: The name of the application, \\\"TimeJob\\\".\\n`title_activity_adicionar`: The title of the \\\"ActivityAdicionar\\\" activity.\",\"/app/src/main/java/net/developermaster/timejob/core/ComponentsMainActivity.kt\":\"This file appears to be a Kotlin code for an Android application that uses Jetpack Compose to create a user interface. It contains several classes and functions that are responsible for different aspects of the application\\u0027s UI.\\n\\n`ComponentsMainActivity`: This class contains the main UI components of the application, including the Scaffold, Home, ImagemPerfil, BottomBar, Fab, and CaixaDeTextoOutLineTextField.\\n\\n`Scaffold`: This function sets up the overall structure of the application\\u0027s UI, including the top bar, bottom bar, and floating action button.\\n\\n`Home`: This function creates the main content area of the application, including a row for the user\\u0027s profile image and a lazy column for displaying a list of items.\\n\\n`ImagemPerfil`: This function creates the user\\u0027s profile image, which is displayed in the Home function.\\n\\n`BottomBar`: This function creates the bottom navigation bar, which includes icons for different parts of the application.\\n\\n`Fab`: This function creates the floating action button, which is used to navigate to the ActivityAdicionar screen.\\n\\n`CaixaDeTextoOutLineTextField`: This function creates a text input field with a border and leading and trailing icons.\",\"/app/src/main/java/net/developermaster/timejob/view/ui/theme/Type.kt\":\"This file defines the typography styles for a Jetpack Compose-based Android application. It sets the default text style for the `bodyLarge` component, and provides commented-out examples for other text styles such as `titleLarge` and `labelSmall`.\\n\\n`Typography`: Defines the set of Material typography styles to be used in the application.\",\"/build.gradle.kts\":\"This file is a top-level build file that sets up common configuration options for all sub-projects or modules in the Android application. It does not contain any classes or functions, but rather sets up the necessary plugins and dependencies for the project, including the Google services Gradle plugin for Firebase integration.\",\"/app/src/main/java/net/developermaster/timejob/core/ComponentsFireBase.kt\":\"This file contains a single class `ComponentsFireBase` that provides two composable functions: `Salvar()` and `ListarTodos()`.\\n\\n`ComponentsFireBase`: A class that handles the UI and Firebase integration for a time job tracking application.\\n\\n`Salvar()`: A composable function that creates a UI for saving a new time job entry to Firebase Firestore.\\n\\n`ListarTodos()`: A composable function that creates a UI for displaying a list of all the time job entries stored in Firebase Firestore.\",\"/app/src/test/java/net/developermaster/timejob/ExampleUnitTest.kt\":\"This file contains a single unit test class with one test function. The file does not define any classes or functions, but rather contains a single unit test that verifies the correctness of the addition operation.\\n\\n`ExampleUnitTest`: A unit test class that contains a single test case to verify the correctness of the addition operation.\\n\\n`addition_isCorrect()`: A test function that checks if the addition of 2 and 2 is equal to 4, which is the expected result.\",\"/README.md\":\"This file appears to be a document describing an app called \\\"TimeJob\\\" for tracking time at work. There are no classes or functions listed in the provided text. The file seems to be a high-level description of the app\\u0027s purpose, which is to provide an app for tracking time spent at work.\"}"}
